question_title: How many MPs will Reform UK have on May 7, 2026?
summary: Reform UK currently has 8 MPs (5 elected July 2024 + Kruger Sep 2025 + Jenrick Jan 15 + Rosindell Jan 18 + Braverman Jan 26, 2026). The question asks about May 7, 2026 - about 3 months away. Key dynamics: (1) Farage has set May 7 as a hard deadline for defections, creating urgency; (2) Reform leads polls massively (+11 over Labour, +14 over Conservatives); (3) MRP forecasts project Conservatives down to 45-70 seats, giving strong incentive to defect; (4) 3 defections in 11 days in January shows momentum; BUT (5) there's been a ~2 week pause since Braverman; (6) the most willing/prominent defectors have likely already gone; (7) "nothing ever happens" applies - most MPs will stay. My central estimate is 9-10 MPs, with the mode at 8-9 and meaningful right-tail risk of a larger wave before the deadline.

factors.description: May 7 deadline explicitly set by Farage creates urgency for wavering Tory MPs - any defection must happen before this date
Reform massively leads polls (+11 over Labour, +14 over Conservatives); MRP forecasts project Reform winning 381 seats
3 sitting MP defections in 11 days in January demonstrates strong momentum and lowers barrier for others
~2 week pause since Braverman defection (Jan 26) - initial burst may have exhausted the most willing defectors
Most high-profile right-wing Conservative figures have already defected or are not sitting MPs (e.g., Rees-Mogg lost his seat)
By-election criticism and party loyalty create reputational costs for defectors, slowing further movement
Very low probability of losing MPs (death, resignation from Reform) in 3-month window - floor is essentially 8

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve as the number of MPs in the British House of Commons who are members of Reform UK on May 7, 2026.
fine_print:
This question will primarily resolve based upon official [UK Parliament Records](parliament.uk). If the records are unavailable or found to be unreliable, credible sources may also be used.

If Reform UK changes its name, the new name will still qualify as Reform UK for the purpose of this question.

***
This question's information (resolution criteria, fine print, background info, etc) is synced with an [original identical question](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42026) which opened on 2026-02-05 05:01:19. However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled. Additionally, if the original question's resolution could have been known before this question opened, then this question will be annulled.
background_info:
Reform UK is a British political party founded in 2018 as the Brexit Party, renamed in January 2021. The party is led by Nigel Farage, who serves as MP for Clacton.

At the July 2024 general election, Reform UK won 5 seats. Since then, several Conservative MPs have defected:

* Robert Jenrick (Newark) - January 16, 2026
* Andrew Rosindell (Romford) - January 18, 2026
* Suella Braverman (Fareham and Waterlooville) - January 26, 2026

As of late January 2026, Reform UK has 8 MPs in the House of Commons. The party has publicly stated that May 7, 2026 (the date of the upcoming local elections) is the cutoff date for accepting defecting MPs and councillors.
